title: Student's t-Distribution: A Bridge Between Normal and Real-World Data
description: A visual explanation of how the Student's t-distribution relates to the normal distribution, its key properties (heavy tails, degrees of freedom), and its primary application in machine learning for hypothesis testing.
elements: Sample Data with high uncertainty (small n)
Hypothesis Test Decision (Reject H0 / Fail to Reject H0)
relationships: As sample size (n) decreases, uncertainty increases, and the t-distribution has heavier tails than the normal distribution to account for this extra uncertainty.
The calculated t-statistic is compared against the t-distribution to determine the probability (p-value) of observing the sample mean if the null hypothesis (e.g., μ = 0) is true.
As Degrees of Freedom (ν) increase, the t-distribution converges and becomes nearly identical to the standard normal distribution.
